headline: Apple Towson Closure Puts Retail Labor Relations In Investor Focus
summary: Apple is closing its first unionized U.S. retail store in Towson, Maryland, citing deteriorating mall conditions and the exit of other retailers. The Towson store had been a milestone location for organized labor within Apple’s U.S. retail network. Employees are not being offered direct transfers as part of the closure plan, which affects how the move is being viewed by staff and labor organizers. For investors following NasdaqGS:AAPL, this development sits alongside Apple’s core business...

headline: Apple leads global smartphone shipments in first quarter, Counterpoint says

(Reporting ​by Ananya Palyekar ​in ‌Bengaluru; Editing ​by Sahal ​Muhammed)

headline: TSMC revenue surges 35% on AI chip demand
summary:
STORY: :: TSMC

The world's largest contract chipmaker, TSMC, saw first-quarter revenue surge 35% thanks to the AI boom. 

Revenue from January-March hit $35.7 billion, beating market forecasts on surging interest in artificial intelligence applications.

Friday's results are in line with TSMC's January guidance of up to to $35.8 billion on its last earnings call.

The tech titan will report full first-quarter earnings on April 16, including an updated current quarter and full year outlook.

The company has been a major beneficiary of advances in AI.

That boom has more than offset a tapering-off in pandemic-led demand for chips used in consumer electronics like tablets.

TSMC's customers include Nvidia and Apple.

:: TSMC

Its shares closed up more than 2% on Friday.

:: TSMC

Its shares closed up more than 2% on Friday.

headline: Apple leads smartphone market even as overall shipments decline, Counterpoint says
summary: IPhone-maker Apple led global smartphone shipments in the first quarter of ‌2026, growing 5% year-on-year, even as ‌overall shipments fell due to a shortage of memory components, ​Counterpoint Research said on Friday.  Global smartphone shipments fell 6% from a year earlier as Middle East tensions also weighed on consumer sentiment, the ‌research firm said.  * ⁠Apple led the smartphone market for the first time in afirst quarter ⁠with a 21% market share, Counterpoint said.

content.title: Apple leads smartphone market even as overall shipments decline, Counterpoint says
content.summary: IPhone-maker Apple led global smartphone shipments in the first quarter of ‌2026, growing 5% year-on-year, even as ‌overall shipments fell due to a shortage of memory components, ​Counterpoint Research said on Friday.  Global smartphone shipments fell 6% from a year earlier as Middle East tensions also weighed on consumer sentiment, the ‌research firm said.  * ⁠Apple led the smartphone market for the first time in afirst quarter ⁠with a 21% market share, Counterpoint said.

headline: Anthropic Has Banks, Regulators Rattled Over AI Cyber Risks. Some Big Names Could Benefit.
summary: Cybersecurity stocks could be set for a massive boost from risks tied to Anthropic’s latest advances in artificial intelligence, which have stoked concerns in Washington and on Wall Street that a new generation of hackers could test the defenses of the country’s biggest banks.  Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, as well as Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, arranged an emergency meeting of top bank bosses earlier this week, Bloomberg reported on Friday, to address the risks raised by the limited release of Anthropic’s newest AI model, known as Claude Mythos.  Each of the lenders invited, according to the report, are part of the so-called Big 8 group of Global Systemically Important Banks, which the Fed deems crucial to broader financial system risks and imposes higher capital buffer rules as backstop.

content.title: Anthropic Has Banks, Regulators Rattled Over AI Cyber Risks. Some Big Names Could Benefit.
content.summary: Cybersecurity stocks could be set for a massive boost from risks tied to Anthropic’s latest advances in artificial intelligence, which have stoked concerns in Washington and on Wall Street that a new generation of hackers could test the defenses of the country’s biggest banks.  Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, as well as Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, arranged an emergency meeting of top bank bosses earlier this week, Bloomberg reported on Friday, to address the risks raised by the limited release of Anthropic’s newest AI model, known as Claude Mythos.  Each of the lenders invited, according to the report, are part of the so-called Big 8 group of Global Systemically Important Banks, which the Fed deems crucial to broader financial system risks and imposes higher capital buffer rules as backstop.

headline: March inflation: Egg prices may be down, but are other costs moving?
summary: Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman and Yahoo Finance Head of News Myles Udland go through various consumer categories and examine how March inflation pressures have pushed their prices higher. Last month's Consumer Price Index survey (CPI) reported to have risen 0.9% month-over-month and 3.3% year-over-year (vs. estimates of 3.4%). Core CPI — which excludes food and energy costs — saw inflation rise 0.2% monthly (vs. estimates of 0.3%) and 2.6% annually (vs. estimates of 2.7%) in March.

content.title: March inflation: Egg prices may be down, but are other costs moving?
content.description:
Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman and Yahoo Finance Head of News Myles Udland go through various consumer categories and examine how March inflation pressures have pushed their prices higher.

Last month's Consumer Price Index survey (CPI) reported to have risen 0.9% month-over-month and 3.3% year-over-year (vs. estimates of 3.4%). Core CPI — which excludes food and energy costs — saw inflation rise 0.2% monthly (vs. estimates of 0.3%) and 2.6% annually (vs. estimates of 2.7%) in March.

headline: Anthropic’s New AI Model Triggered an Emergency Banking Meeting. It’s a Reason to Buy Cybersecurity Stocks.
summary: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, as well as Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, arranged an emergency meeting of top bank bosses earlier this week, Bloomberg reported on Friday, to address the risks raised by the limited release of Anthropic’s newest AI model, known as Claude Mythos.  Each of the lenders invited, according to the report, are part of the so-called Big 8 group of Global Systemically Important Banks, which the Fed deems crucial to broader financial system risks and imposes higher capital buffer rules as backstop.  The concern stems from the ability of Mythos to find and exploit so-called zero-day vulnerabilities in tech infrastructure, based on relatively simple prompting.

content.title: Anthropic’s New AI Model Triggered an Emergency Banking Meeting. It’s a Reason to Buy Cybersecurity Stocks.
content.summary: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, as well as Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, arranged an emergency meeting of top bank bosses earlier this week, Bloomberg reported on Friday, to address the risks raised by the limited release of Anthropic’s newest AI model, known as Claude Mythos.  Each of the lenders invited, according to the report, are part of the so-called Big 8 group of Global Systemically Important Banks, which the Fed deems crucial to broader financial system risks and imposes higher capital buffer rules as backstop.  The concern stems from the ability of Mythos to find and exploit so-called zero-day vulnerabilities in tech infrastructure, based on relatively simple prompting.

headline: Wall Street Banks Try Out Anthropic’s Mythos as US Urges Testing
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Wall Street banks are starting to test Anthropic PBC’s Mythos model internally as Trump administration officials encourage them to use it to detect vulnerabilities.While JPMorgan Chase & Co. was the only bank named as part of an initiative to test the Mythos model, other major financial institutions have also gained access or expect to in the coming days, according to people familiar with the matter. Goldman Sachs Group Inc., Citigroup Inc., Bank of America Corp. and Morgan Stanle

content.summary: (Bloomberg) -- Wall Street banks are starting to test Anthropic PBC’s Mythos model internally as Trump administration officials encourage them to use it to detect vulnerabilities.While JPMorgan Chase & Co. was the only bank named as part of an initiative to test the Mythos model, other major financial institutions have also gained access or expect to in the coming days, according to people familiar with the matter. Goldman Sachs Group Inc., Citigroup Inc., Bank of America Corp. and Morgan Stanle
